title: impatiens roylei
intro: Impatiens roylei, also known as the 'Balsam', is a perennial flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of glycosides and phenolic compounds in its roots, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Impatiens roylei can be made by infusing the roots in water or using them in combination with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, or decoctions for medicinal use.
